title: Survival Chess
subtitle: Checks, captures, threats, loose pieces, safe kings, and the habits that stop beginner losses.
purpose: Stop the first wave of beginner losses: hanging pieces, missed checks, unsafe kings, careless trades, and stalemating won games.
audience: Players who know the rules but lose material for free
School learners moving beyond legal moves
Chess99 users starting daily tactical practice
howToRead: Solve each diagram before reading the caption.
Say checks, captures, threats before every guided move.
Do the checkpoint without hints before moving to the next chapter.
outcomes: Use a checks-captures-threats scan before moving.
Recognize hanging pieces and defended pieces.
Answer one-move attacks with simple defenses.
Escape check using safe king squares.
Convert queen and rook mates without stalemate.
Perform a final blunder-check before every move.

assetPolicy.authoritativeDiagrams: Generate from FEN, arrows, highlights, and board theme only.
assetPolicy.generativeImages: Allowed for cover and concept art only, never for exact chess positions.

chapterOrder.title: The Three Questions: Checks, Captures, Threats
Capturing Wisely: Good Trades And Bad Trades
Basic Checkmates With Queen And Rook
Opening Safety: Stop Moving The Same Piece
Ten Survival Games: Find The Safe Move

assessment.unlockRule: Complete the guided survival move and answer all checkpoint questions correctly.
